MOTION FOR LEAVE TO File First Amended Complaint
Off Calendar Jun-13-2023 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES:
Atty. TRICIA L. LEGITTINO, Esq. of Frankfurt Kurnit Klein + Selz PC (Tel: 310-579-9600) for plaintiff;
Atty. LIAM K. MALONE, Esq. of Litchfield Law PC (Tel: 949-520-7570) for defendants Hammel, Green and Abrahamson, Inc.

HEARING RE: PLAINTIFF JOHN STEPHEN GRAHAM, JR.'s Motion for Leave to File First Amended Complaint.

Matter argued and submitted. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Plaintiff's motion for leave to amend the complaint is denied.

"Although courts are bound to apply a policy of great liberality in permitting amendments to the complaint at any stage of the proceedings, up to and including trial, this policy should be applied only where no prejudice is shown to the adverse party." (Atkinson v. Elk Corp. (2003) 109 Cal.App.4th 739, 761.) Prejudice exists where the amendment would result in a delay of trial along with added cost of preparation and increased burden of discovery. (Edmon & Karnow, CALIFORNIA PRACTICE GUIDE: CIVIL PROCEDURE BEFORE TRIAL 6:656 (Rutter 2022).)

Adding six causes of action, one plaintiff, and one previously-dismissed defendant would surely increase the burden of discovery, and result in the delay of an already delayed trial date only two months away. With diligence, these claims could have been added months ago.

Order signed.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr.; Clerk: Sean Kane; Court Reporter: Angela Kott, CSR No. 7811, Email: abkott@sbcglobal.net; Tel: 415-706-2594 (302/RBU)

MOTION FOR LEAVE TO File A Cross-Complaint (Amended)
Off Calendar Jun-13-2023 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES:
Atty. TRICIA L. LEGITTINO, Esq. of Frankfurt Kurnit Klein + Selz PC (Tel: 310-579-9600) for plaintiff;
Atty. LIAM K. MALONE, Esq. of Litchfield Law PC (Tel: 949-520-7570) for defendants Hammel, Green and Abrahamson, Inc.

HEARING RE: DEFENDANT HAMMEL, GREEN AND ABRAHAMSON, INC.'s Motion for Leave to File A Cross-Complaint.

Matter argued and submitted. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Defendant's motion for leave to file a cross-complaint is denied.

The already delayed trial date is two months away and adding new parties would no doubt delay it again. (See Crocker Nat. Bank v. Emerald (1990) 221 Cal.App.3d 852, 864 [leave for cross-complaint denied when "filed only five months before the trial date"].) The parties bicker about why, but, with diligence, cross-claims could have been filed many months ago. In any event, the equitable indemnity claims would survive the trial. (See Expressions at Rancho Niguel Assn. v. Ahmanson Development, Inc. (2001) 86 Cal.App.4th 1133, 1139.)

Order signed.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr.; Clerk: Sean Kane; Court Reporter: Angela Kott, CSR No. 7811, Email: abkott@sbcglobal.net; Tel: 415-706-2594 (302/RBU)

Melissa Palozola, Esq. (415.984.8500; MPalozola@ClarkHill.com) of Clark Hill LLP for Plaintiff ( via Zoom Videoconference.)
Sharon Sanner Muir, Esq. (949.520.7570; sharon@litchfield.law) of Litchfield Law PC for Defendants.

ARGUED The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANTS' HAMMEL GREEN and ABRAHAMSON, INC.'S DEMURRER to COMPLAINT.

The demurrer is ordered off calendar for noncompliance with CCP 430.41. In Exhibit A to her declarations, defense counsel states: "We have not met and conferred regarding a potential demurrer" Another declaration refers to "meet and confer efforts," but does state the "means" by which the meet and confer occurred, as the statute requires. The parties are ordered to meet and confer immediately and defendant's response to the complaint is now due by July 25, 2022.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr., Clerk: S. Le, Not Reported. =(302/RBU)
